occurrence_sentence: Today Microsoft announced a game-changing collaboration to extend our lead in the race: together we will build the world's most powerful quantum computer on a path to scientific and commercial advantage.

Microsoft and Atom Computing have been working closely together to accelerate the development of fault-tolerant quantum supercomputers that can solve impactful problems too difficult for even the most powerful classical supercomputing systems.

interaction_phrase: announced the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to explore
occurrence_sentence: Atom Computing and quantum algorithms company Phasecraft today announced the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to explore how application-focused algorithms can be used to benchmark progress toward utility-scale quantum computers.

interaction_phrase: will collaborate on accelerating the delivery and adoption of quantum solutions
occurrence_sentence: Through this MOU, the companies will collaborate on accelerating the delivery and adoption of quantum solutions in key application areas, such as the development of materials for batteries and photovoltaics.

interaction_phrase: announced a $100 million Letter of Intent with the U.S. Department of Commerce
occurrence_sentence: Atom Computing continues to lead the quantum computing industry through its pioneering work in neutral-atom quantum technology. Phasecraft works in partnership with leading quantum hardware companies, including Google, IBM, Quantinuum, and QuEra, academic and industry leaders, to develop high-efficiency algorithms to move quantum computing from experimental demonstrations to useful applications.
Phasecraft was founded in 2019 by Toby Cubitt, Ashley Montanaro, and John Morton, expert quantum scientists who have spent decades leading top research teams at UCL and the University of Bristol.
